title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
What's Covered in a Life Insurance Policy and What's Not?
Keywords: life insurance policy, life cover, benefits of life insurance, whole life insurance
Meta Description: Thinking about purchasing a life insurance plan? Read on to know more about what’s covered and not covered by such policies.
From saving for the future to investing in it, a life insurance policy is one of the right instruments for fulfilling your life goals as well as safeguarding the financial future of your loved ones. As per the Swiss Re sigma report, life insurance penetration in India rose to 3.2% in 20211, lodging a rising preference for such policies among Indians.
Despite this favourable attitude towards life insurance policies, there still remains a lot of confusion about the basics of such plans. This in turn leads to many policyholders settling for inadequate life covers and losing out on the benefits of a life insurance plan.
To get a better understanding of what a life insurance policy entails, we must first begin by reviewing what it covers and what it does not cover.
What’s Covered by a Life Insurance Plan?
Whether it be a whole life insurance plan or a ULIP plan, the death of the policyholder is always covered by life insurance plans. Apart from this basic coverage, you can extend the benefits of life insurance plans by opting for appropriate riders.
Common life insurance riders enhance the umbrella of your plan and extend financial benefits in the following instances:
·         If the policyholder is diagnosed with a critical illness
·         If the policyholder sustains partial or permanent disability due to an accident
It is important to note that such additional need-based riders expand your life cover, but against a nominal increase to your premium amount.
What’s Not Covered by a Life Insurance Plan?
Now that we’ve looked at what’s covered by a life insurance plan, let’s review what falls outside the coverage umbrella of such plans. The following types of deaths are generally excluded by life covers:
·         Life insurance policies do not cover death by suicide during the first policy year. .
·         While murder is covered by most insurance policies, it may not be covered if the demise of the policyholder happened due to his involvement in criminal activities or if the individual is found to be involved in a crime.
·         Policyholders may be suffering from certain pre-existing diseases before purchasing a life cover. If that’s the case, this information needs to be shared with the insurance provider. However, life covers generally do not cover the death of the policyholder resulting from such pre-existing conditions.
·         The death of a policyholder resulting from alcohol or any other intoxicant is not covered by a life insurance policy. Additionally, if the policyholder withholds information about certain harmful habits like smoking when purchasing a life cover, deaths resulting from such undisclosed harmful habits won’t be covered by their policies.
·         While statistically, deaths due to childbirth and its allied complications have reduced in recent years, there are still many women who have to endure such a dreaded fate. Life insurance policies do not cover such deaths.
·         Life insurance policies do not cover deaths resulting from extremely dangerous activities like paragliding, hiking, etc. While these may be accidents, they are not covered by such insurance plans.
·         Deaths caused by natural disasters like tsunamis, earthquakes, etc. are not covered by life insurance plans.
·         If the death of the policyholder happens in a terrorist attack, it won’t be covered by a life insurance plan.
Should you Opt for a Life Insurance Plan?
If you’re wondering whether you should opt for a life insurance plan or not, the answer is a definite yes. Despite its exclusions, a life cover is one of the most important safety net you can cast for the financial stability and security of your family.
Not only does a life insurance policy help your loved ones meet their life goals in your absence, but plans like the ULIP also come with additional wealth creation opportunities. You can opt for such unit-linked plans to achieve both your long and short-term life goals. With endowment and whole life insurance plans, you can save funds for your children’s higher education, pay off debts, plan for your retirement, etc.
While all life insurance plans come with a certain set of benefits, you can further extend these benefits by opting for add-on riders. By opting for appropriate riders like a critical illness cover, you get to enjoy exemptions from hospitalisation costs related to such conditions.
Wrapping it Up
While we’ve outlined the general inclusions and exclusions of a life insurance policy, this does not mean that you can skip reading your policy document. The specifics of one life insurance plan may vastly vary from the next. Thus, reading the policy document carefully is important, if you wish to enjoy an optimised life cover.
